---
title: "Global Barrier Project"
subtitle: "Synthesizing animal tracking data to quantify effects of human infrastructure on terrestrial mammal movement"
date: today
---

## About the project

Anthropogenic infrastructure such as settlements, roads, fences, railways is expanding faster
than our ability to measure its cumulative effect on wildlife movement. Single-study
designs miss cross-system patterns. 

We assembled a global GPS tracking dataset for terrestrial mammals to examine how
human infrastructure reshape animal movement —
from fine-scale behavioral responses to landscape-level connectivity loss.

By harmonizing tracking data across species and biomes, we ask:

- How does the configuration, in addition to the composition, of anthropogenic infrastructure shape animal movement?
- How animals cross different types of transporation infrastructure differently?
- How can we use actual animal movement data to inform functional connectivity?

The compiled archive currently spans **301 studies** across **69 species** and
**60+ countries**, contributed by collaborators worldwide.

[See the study sites map →](map.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[Read the data protocol →](protocol.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}

## Team & contributors

To be added.

## Outcome

To be added.
